name: Farsons Beer Festival
description: Malta's biggest beer festival — 10 days of craft beer, live music across 4 stages, food, and summer vibes at Ta' Qali. Featuring Cisk, Blue Label, Guinness, and guest brews.
transport: Multiple bus routes to Ta' Qali National Park
extras: Main stage acts typically kick off around 9:30 – 9:45pm. 4 stages: Main Stage, Rock Stage, Casa Electronica, Craft Beer Area.

name: Balzan Festa — The Annunciation of Mary
description: Balzan's annual village feast celebrating the Annunciation of Mary. A week of street decorations, band marches, and fireworks leading up to the Sunday procession.
transport: Bus routes through Balzan — check Tallinja app for schedules
extras: Friday: medium fireworks display. Saturday evening: main aerial show + ground fireworks around 11pm. Sunday: procession and final aerial display.

name: Lija Festa — Christ Our Saviour
description: One of Malta's most spectacular festas. Lija is legendary for its fireworks — the aerial displays are considered among the best on the island.
transport: Bus routes through Lija — check Tallinja app for schedules
extras: Famous for aerial firework displays — one of Malta's top firework festas. Ground fireworks typically around 11pm on the eve of the feast.

name: Luqa Festa — St Andrew
description: Luqa's parish feast of St Andrew, with traditional street decorations, band marches, and fireworks.
transport: Bus 71, 73 to Luqa — check Tallinja app
extras: Procession on Sunday. Fireworks Saturday evening.

name: Ħamrun Festa — Immaculate Conception
description: One of Ħamrun's two big festas. Passionate celebrations with elaborate street decorations and a strong community turnout.
transport: Bus routes through Ħamrun — very central, easy to reach
extras: Procession on Sunday evening. Fireworks Saturday night.

name: Rabat Festa — St Paul
description: Rabat celebrates its patron St Paul with a traditional village feast. Rabat's narrow streets make for an atmospheric setting.
transport: Bus 51, 52, 53 to Rabat
extras: Procession winds through Rabat's historic streets. Fireworks on Saturday.

name: Fgura Festa — Our Lady of Mount Carmel
description: Fgura's parish feast of Our Lady of Mount Carmel with street decorations, band clubs, and fireworks.
transport: Bus routes to Fgura — check Tallinja app
extras: Standard festa week programme. Fireworks Saturday, procession Sunday.

name: Gżira Festa — Our Lady of Mount Carmel
description: Gżira celebrates Our Lady of Mount Carmel. A smaller festa with a loyal local following along the seafront.
transport: Bus to Gżira — on the Sliema side, very accessible
extras: Fireworks over the harbour make for great views.

name: Valletta Festa — Our Lady of Mount Carmel
description: One of Valletta's grandest festas. The Carmelite Church with its massive dome hosts a spectacular celebration in the heart of the capital.
transport: Any bus to Valletta — the festa is in the main streets
extras: Thursday feast day. The dome is lit up at night — stunning views from Sliema waterfront.

name: Qormi Festa — St Sebastian
description: Qormi's St Sebastian festa is one of Malta's most passionate and intense celebrations. Rival band clubs, massive crowds, and raw festa energy.
transport: Bus routes to Qormi — arrive early, roads close
extras: Known for its intense rivalry with St George's parish. Expect big crowds. Fireworks are top-tier.

name: Mosta Festa — The Holy Family
description: Mosta's summer festa at the iconic Rotunda — one of Malta's most recognisable churches. Great atmosphere in the town square.
transport: Bus 31, 44, 45 to Mosta
extras: The Rotunda dome is the third-largest unsupported dome in the world.

name: Victoria Festa — St George
description: Gozo's capital celebrates St George with one of the island's biggest festas. The Citadel provides a dramatic backdrop.
transport: Gozo Ferry from Ċirkewwa, then bus to Victoria
extras: If you're doing a Gozo day trip, time it around this festa.

name: Żurrieq Festa — Our Lady of Mount Carmel
description: Żurrieq is famous for its massive festa — one of the biggest in the south. Incredible street decorations, passionate crowds, and amazing fireworks.
transport: Bus 71, 73 to Żurrieq — arrive early
extras: One of Malta's top festas for sheer scale. The main square gets packed.

name: Marsaskala Festa — Saint Anne
description: Marsaskala's seaside festa. The waterfront setting adds a different vibe — festa meets beach town.
transport: Bus to Marsaskala — waterfront location
extras: Fireworks over the bay are worth seeing.

name: Birżebbuġa Festa — St Peter in Chains
description: Birżebbuġa's festa is known for its spectacular ground fireworks on the Saturday evening — some of the best on the island.
transport: Bus 82 to Birżebbuġa
extras: Saturday evening ground fireworks are the main attraction — arrive early for a good spot.

name: Marsaxlokk Festa — Our Lady of Pompei
description: Marsaxlokk's fishing village festa. Luzzu boats, seafood stalls, and fireworks over the harbour.
transport: Bus 81 to Marsaxlokk
extras: Combine with the Sunday fish market for the full Marsaxlokk experience.

name: Ħamrun Festa — St Cajetan
description: San Gejtanu is Ħamrun's biggest festa and one of Malta's most passionate celebrations. Known for its extravagance, massive crowds, and intense atmosphere.
transport: Bus routes to Ħamrun — very central
extras: One of the most iconic festas in Malta. Expect huge crowds, long marches, and non-stop energy.

name: Santa Marija — Mosta
description: Santa Marija at the Mosta Rotunda — one of the biggest festas of the year. The 15th of August is a public holiday and Mosta goes all out.
transport: Bus 31, 44, 45 to Mosta — expect heavy traffic
extras: Public holiday. The Rotunda is stunning when lit up. Massive fireworks display.

name: Santa Marija — Mqabba
description: Mqabba's Santa Marija is legendary for fireworks. One of the best aerial displays in Malta — a must-see for any festa lover.
transport: Bus to Mqabba — roads close early, use public transport
extras: Legendary fireworks — rival to Lija for best on the island. Go for the Saturday night display.

name: Santa Marija — Attard
description: Attard's Santa Marija festa. A more relaxed village feast near the Presidential gardens with lovely decorations.
transport: Bus routes to Attard
extras: Close to San Anton Gardens — nice combo for a visit.

name: Santa Marija — Qrendi
description: Qrendi's Santa Marija is another top festa. Strong community, great fireworks, and one of the most recommended festas for visitors.
transport: Bus to Qrendi
extras: Another strong fireworks festa. Close to Ħaġar Qim temples if you want a daytime visit too.

name: Santa Marija — Victoria, Gozo
description: Gozo's Santa Marija in Victoria. The Citadel lit up for the feast is one of the most beautiful sights in the Maltese islands.
transport: Gozo Ferry + bus to Victoria
extras: Worth the ferry trip. The whole town is decorated.

name: Sliema Festa — Stella Maris
description: Sliema's Stella Maris festa along the seafront. A more urban festa with a mix of locals and tourists.
transport: Bus to Sliema — Stella Maris church area
extras: Fireworks over the sea, visible from the whole Sliema waterfront.

name: Għargħur Festa — St Bartholomew
description: A charming hilltop village festa in Għargħur. Small but atmospheric, with great views of the island.
transport: Bus to Għargħur
extras: Smaller festa with a local feel. Worth it for the hilltop atmosphere.

name: St Julian's Festa — St Julian
description: St Julian's celebrates its patron saint. Spinola Bay lit up with decorations makes for a great setting.
transport: Bus to St Julian's — Spinola Bay area
extras: Combine with dinner at Spinola Bay. Fireworks over the bay.

name: Mellieħa Festa — Nativity of Our Lady
description: Mellieħa's Victory Day festa on September 8th — a public holiday. One of the last big festas of the season and always a strong celebration.
transport: Bus to Mellieħa
extras: September 8th is Victory Day — public holiday. Massive celebrations across multiple towns.

name: Naxxar Festa — Nativity of Our Lady
description: Naxxar's massive September 8th festa. One of the biggest celebrations for Victory Day with spectacular decorations and huge crowds.
transport: Bus to Naxxar
extras: Naxxar goes big for this one. The parish square is fully transformed.

name: Senglea Festa — Nativity of Our Lady
description: Senglea's Victory Day festa in the Three Cities. The narrow streets and harbour views make this one atmospheric.
transport: Bus to Senglea or ferry from Valletta
extras: The Gardjola Gardens viewpoint is stunning during the festa.

name: Żabbar Festa — Our Lady of Graces
description: Żabbar's festa is one of the last big ones of the season and it goes hard. Passionate crowds, elaborate decorations, and a grand procession.
transport: Bus to Żabbar
extras: One of the biggest festas of September. Strong closing to the season.
